parsed.rationale: Backend shows clean layering: routes/ → services/ → db/ with a dedicated policy/ module that centralizes all business constants (MAX_ACTIVE_LOANS=5, LOAN_PERIOD_DAYS=14, LATE_FINE_RATE_CENTS_PER_DAY=50, LATE_FINE_CAP_CENTS=1000, FINE_BORROW_BLOCK_THRESHOLD_CENTS=500, RESERVATION_NOTIFICATION_HOURS=48) in policy/index.ts:7-12 and exposes them as pure functions (computeFineCents, canBorrow, calculateDueDate, calculateReservationExpiry). A small named error hierarchy (NotFoundError, ConflictError, ForbiddenError, PaymentRequiredError in errors.ts) is used consistently — the loan handler maps them to HTTP codes (loan-handler.ts:24-31) and the service throws them (loan-service.ts:104-118). madge.json shows zero circular deps and zero orphans. File-size distribution is healthy: largest file is loanService.ts at 262 lines; everything else is ≤238; no file exceeds 300. Frontend has a clear pages/ + components/ + hooks/ + types/ split with one shared types/index.ts and clean route definitions. This meets every level-3 criterion. It does NOT reach level 4: (a) services call raw db.prepare with SQL inline throughout loan-service.ts (lines 26, 32, 38, 56, 75, 84, 92, 139, 168, etc.) — there is no thin DB-access abstraction, so services are not unit-testable in isolation; (b) the frontend is organized by technical role (components/, pages/, hooks/, contexts/) rather than by feature (no checkout/, members/, books/ feature folders) — a CheckoutPage and its confirm step live in pages/CheckoutPage.tsx alongside unrelated pages, with related logic sprawled into hooks/useApi.ts and contexts/MemberContext.tsx; (c) shared types are duplicated — frontend/src/types/index.ts (63 lines) re-declares domain shapes rather than importing from a shared workspace or generated client. The reservation state machine is split between policy helpers (nextReservationToNotify, isReservationExpired) and reservationService transitions rather than living in a single owning module.
